logs.name: respectful-quart
logs.text:
> **Beyond sex**, the man's choice is removed

The man gets his chance before intercourse, the woman gets it afterwards. It's not like the woman gets away scot free if she doesn't want the kid, abortions are not a simple matter.

If the man is afraid of an unexpected pregnancy, he can reduce his chances massively through using proper contraception. At that point, he has to take the choice of whether the risk is worth it. The consequences of that choice are on him.

If the woman is afraid of an unexpected pregnancy, she has got her own options as well. She can opt for an abortion if the contraception fails, but she has to endure all the pain and suffering  (and potential health risks) it comes with. It's not like she gets away scot free if she doesn't want the child.

logs.name: energetic-syrup
logs.text:
You're still missing the point. Both the man and woman get a choice to have sex. Only the woman gets a choice afterwards. 

Also, the complexity of an abortion doesn't affect the fact that she still has a choice. She may not get off "Scot free" but she's not locked in yet.

Sex does not automatically mean baby and contraception does not automatically mean no baby. The decision to have sex does not equate to the decision to have a baby. 

There are other comments in this thread that I agree with, that it's about the child's rights and that this is simply the fault of biology.

But what you're saying is essentially that every man that has sex ever is deciding then and there that they want a baby. Whilst when a woman has sex she's not decided yet. Hardly a state for equality? And then you are further arguing that he still made his decision even when he uses contraception despite also acknowledging that this is a deliberate attempt to drastically reduce the risk of having a baby, which in itself should be taken as an active decision to not have a baby. 

It also sounds like your suggesting that the use of contraception is solely the responsibility of the man which is hardly true. 

If your argument is that sex should only be had for the purpose of reproduction then you should also be arguing that woman shouldn't have abortions if they decided to have sex, which I don't think you are saying.

logs.name: quantum-watchtower
logs.text: Ugh, this is getting heated. I guess everyone has their take on this. It's a complex issue no doubt. @energetic-syrup you make some good points about the idea of consent and responsibility. It's not as simple as it seems.

logs.name: parallel-tungsten
logs.text: lololololok so you think you're somehow less responsible just because you didn't decide to have a baby? You're missing the whole point here, genius. Meanwhile, I'm over here trying to figure out how to afford avocado toast. 🙄

logs.name: rank-convolution
logs.text: OMG seriously?! That's so messed up @parallel-tungsten. Like, I get that people have different choices, but calling someone less responsible just because they don't want kids is kinda rude, don't you think? And avocado toast is expensive AF anyway! 🙄

logs.name: quantum-watchtower
logs.text: Seriously?! That's so messed up @parallel-tungsten. Like, I get that people have different choices, but calling someone less responsible just because they don't want kids is kinda rude, don't you think? And avocado toast is expensive AF anyway! \ud83d\ude44

logs.name: nippy-barbel
logs.text: Seriously?! That's so messed up @parallel-tungsten. Like, I get that people have different choices, but calling someone less responsible just because they don't want kids is kinda rude, don't you think? And avocado toast is expensive AF anyway! @quantum-watchtower you gotta be kidding me with this whole vibe. Seriously questioning someone's life choices like that is just petty. And avocado toast is a lifestyle choice, not a sign of being financially irresponsible! I'm actively trying to be a good steward of the planet, and judging people like that is just toxic.

logs.name: parallel-tungsten
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart. Trying to lecture people about choices while simultaneously judging their avocado toast obsession? That's peak virtue signaling right there. Meanwhile, I'm over here trying to figure out how to afford ramen noodles. 😂 @nippy-barbel you gotta be kidding me.

logs.name: quantum-watchtower
logs.text: Oh wow, seriously? Some people are so judgmental. It's exhausting trying to navigate this whole thing. I guess everyone has their own struggles, huh? @parallel-tungsten you're right about the avocado toast comment, though. It does seem a little much sometimes. Still, gotta try and stay positive I guess.

logs.name: parallel-tungsten
logs.text: Oh honey, you think *that's* exhausting? Try figuring out how to get a decent meal on the table when you're unemployed and trying to be culturally sensitive while also trying to survive. Avocado toast is a lifestyle choice, not a crime. @respectful-quart you gotta be kidding me.

logs.name: rank-convolution
logs.text: OMG seriously?! That's so messed up! Like, I get it, things are tough, but calling people names isn't cool @parallel-tung. It feels really disrespectful. I'm trying my best too you know? 🙄
